pattern.title: Hearsay: kuulemma, muka, näköjään
pattern.summary: Three little words tell your listener how you know something: 'kuulemma' = I heard it from someone, 'näköjään' = I can see the evidence, and 'muka' = supposedly - and I doubt it. Drop them into a sentence and you never have to say 'someone told me that...'
examples: Se on kuulemma tosi hyvä. = It's apparently really good.
Nyt sataa näköjään taas. = It's evidently raining again.
Se muka unohti koko jutun. = They supposedly forgot the whole thing.

sentences.fi: Se uus ravintola on kuulemma tosi hyvä.
sentences.en: That new restaurant is apparently really good.
sentences.written: Se uusi ravintola on kuulemma tosi hyvä.

glosses.näköjään: evidently / by the looks of it

sentences.en: You've evidently been hard at work.

glosses.näköjään: evidently - I can see the results

sentences.en: They supposedly forgot the whole thing.

glosses.se: they / he / she - spoken Finnish uses 'se' for people

sentences.en: Apparently it's supposed to snow tomorrow.

glosses.on: have - spoken Finnish uses 'on' for plurals too

sentences.en: Apparently the meeting moved to next week.

glosses.viikkoon: to week - 'ens viikkoon' = to next week

sentences.fi: Se oli muka koko ajan sairaana.
sentences.en: They were supposedly sick the whole time.
sentences.written: Hän oli muka koko ajan sairaana.

glosses.se: they / he / she - spoken Finnish uses 'se' for people
glosses.muka: supposedly - and I don't quite buy it
